title: Centralized Configuration with Auto-Generated Documentation
description: Implemented emoji legend as JSON SSOT with Python script that auto-generates markdown documentation. Ensures consistency across all system components.
context: Needed consistent emoji usage across threads, lists, commands. Manual synchronization would be error-prone.
outcome: Single source of truth (P2) with human-readable docs. Easy to maintain, extend, and validate.

title: Noun-First Title Structure for UI Constraints
description: Designed thread titles with entity-first structure (e.g., 'CRM Refactor' not 'Refactoring CRM') to optimize for collapsed sidebar where first ~24 chars visible.
context: User's UI shows limited characters (date + emoji + ~24 chars). Verb-first titles waste precious space on low-value words.
outcome: Front-loads critical information. Users can scan quickly even in collapsed view. 18-30 char target fits perfectly.

title: Dual Title Generation for Thread Continuity
description: Generate both current thread title AND next thread title during export. Next title stored in RESUME.md and displayed prominently for copy/paste.
context: User manually starts new threads by loading RESUME from exported thread. Sequential numbering (#1, #2, #3) required manual tracking.
outcome: Automatic sequence increment, consistent 🔗 chain emoji usage, pre-populated next title reduces cognitive load.

title: Automated Mode Bypassing Interactive Features
description: Initial integration failed because --yes flag (automation mode) skipped title generation entirely, jumping to timestamp fallback. Fixed by making title generation work in both interactive AND automated modes.
context: When Zo runs commands on behalf of user, uses --yes for non-interactive execution. Original code treated this as 'skip all generation'.
outcome: Title generation now auto-selects first option in --yes mode instead of falling back to timestamp. Works for both interactive and automated workflows.
